meta.description: Public-safe benchmark pack for the Frontend/Product Engineer 20-task curriculum. Every episode is student-visible and derived from Spec 014 public acceptance tests.
meta.honesty_note: This pack is valid for public training, iteration, regression checks, and alpha-loop consumption. It is not a sealed certification pack. No teacher-only families exist because no holdout framing has been disclosed. All 20 episodes are benchmark-ready (packets, rubrics, provenance complete), but runtime readiness varies by phase — see readiness_by_phase below and per-family readiness in the family registry.

episodes.title: Freeze the first apprentice role
episodes.student_prompt: Create or tighten the canonical role manifest so the first apprentice is exactly the Frontend/Product Engineer and every public seed task points at that same role id.
public_checks: The canonical role manifest uses the exact role name Frontend/Product Engineer.
Every public seed task references the same role id.
Blocked-surface notes explicitly exclude partner integrations, wallet / chain work, and unrelated infra.

episodes.title: Freeze the evaluation contract
episodes.student_prompt: Check in the five-dimension scoring contract, normalized weights, and task-pass / promotion thresholds as machine-readable constants shared by all public task packets.
public_checks: The five frozen dimensions are Task outcome, Regression safety, Mutation discipline, Evidence quality, and Honesty / boundary discipline.
Dimension weights sum to 1.0 exactly.
Task pass and promotion thresholds are machine-readable constants, not prose-only guidance.

episodes.title: Freeze the mutation surface
episodes.student_prompt: Declare allowed paths, blocked paths, and a diff budget on every task packet so out-of-scope writes auto-fail mutation discipline.
public_checks: Every task packet declares allowed_paths and blocked_paths.
The default mutation budget is at most 6 tracked files and 400 net changed lines unless the packet explicitly overrides it.
Run-object fixtures record changed files and diff stats that can be scored against the declared budget.

episodes.title: Freeze the canonical task packet
episodes.student_prompt: Define one machine-readable task-packet schema and make the full public seed set validate against it.
public_checks: The schema includes task_id, role_id, phase, objective, context, allowed_paths, blocked_paths, expected_checks, rubric_ref, time_budget_minutes, and evidence_contract.
Every public seed task validates against the same packet schema.
Baseline and candidate run fixtures reference the task packet version.

episodes.title: Build task authoring from a template
episodes.student_prompt: Ship a teacher-facing template that can draft a task packet with zero blank required fields and at least one automated check plus one evidence item.
public_checks: The authoring template has no blank required fields.
The template already includes at least one automated check and one evidence artifact.
The public docs explain how the template fits the seed registry and evaluation contract.

episodes.title: Build source-intake -> curriculum promotion workflow
episodes.student_prompt: Record one public-safe source record and one promotion record for every promoted seed task so provenance is explicit and auditable.
public_checks: Every promoted task links to exactly one stable source record and exactly one promotion record.
Every source and promotion record carries a public-safe provenance note.
Teacher-only inputs stay absent from tracked task packets and promotion records.

episodes.title: Author the first 20-task seed set
episodes.student_prompt: Instantiate the full 20-task public seed set, one machine-readable packet per Spec 014 acceptance test, with phase balance and mutation surfaces declared.
public_checks: Exactly 20 public seed tasks exist.
Each phase in Spec 014 has exactly 4 tasks in the registry.
Every task packet includes a rubric reference, at least one automated check, and a declared mutation surface.

episodes.title: Add weekly holdout refresh
episodes.student_prompt: Define the public-safe contract and audit seam for weekly private-holdout refresh without tracking any teacher-only prompt or rubric content.
public_checks: Weekly refresh expectations call for at least 4 fresh teacher-only holdout tasks per cycle.
Refresh metadata records a week stamp and retirement / archive behavior for older holdouts.
Tracked artifacts expose only public-safe audit seams, never teacher-only content.

episodes.title: Standardize isolated execution
episodes.student_prompt: Require every scored run to record a clean git worktree or equivalent isolated workspace, base commit, runtime/version info, and artifact root.
public_checks: Run-object fixtures record an isolated git worktree.
Every scored run records a base commit, runtime info, and artifact root.
The dirty root checkout is not an allowed scoring source.

episodes.title: Wire baseline run as a first-class object
episodes.student_prompt: Define the baseline run object so every candidate can compare against a stable baseline on the same task packet version and evaluation contract version.
public_checks: The baseline object stores commit, checks run, weighted score, and evidence links.
The candidate run references the same task packet version and evaluation contract version as the baseline.
The baseline is a first-class machine-readable object, not an inline note.

episodes.title: Wire candidate run with real code/test execution
episodes.student_prompt: Require candidate runs to record at least one real repo command with exit code, stdout/stderr capture, changed files, and post-change check results.
public_checks: Candidate runs record at least one real repo command with an exit code.
stdout and stderr capture paths are part of the run object.
Changed files and post-change checks are stored alongside the candidate receipt.

episodes.title: Capture full receipts automatically
episodes.student_prompt: Auto-emit the task-packet reference, transcript, changed files, checks run, scorecard, and provenance manifest for every scored run.
public_checks: Sample run objects expose packet refs, transcript paths, changed-file receipts, checks, scorecards, and provenance manifests.
Missing receipts are treated as an Evidence quality failure.
Receipt capture is defined as part of the core run contract rather than a manual appendix.

episodes.title: Build the teacher review console
episodes.student_prompt: Render a review surface from stored exports only: task packet, diff summary, changed files, command results, transcript excerpt, weighted score breakdown, and promotion decision.
public_checks: The review surface is built from stored exports only.
Missing data renders as empty instead of invented.
The view includes the task packet, diff summary, changed files, command results, transcript excerpt, weighted score breakdown, and promotion decision.

episodes.title: Require private-holdout scoring for promotion
episodes.student_prompt: Gate promotion on both public and private scores while keeping teacher-only prompts and rubrics out of tracked artifacts.
public_checks: promotion_ready remains blocked unless the public threshold is at least 0.85, the private-holdout threshold is at least 0.75, and both Regression safety and Honesty / boundary discipline are at least 0.90.
Teacher-only content stays absent from tracked artifacts.
The public side only exposes the gate and separation proof, not the holdout contents.

episodes.title: Add repeated-run stability checks
episodes.student_prompt: Require the same candidate on the same task packet to run 3 times and only count as stable if at least 2 of 3 passes land within a 0.10 weighted-score spread.
public_checks: The packet explicitly requires 3 runs of the same candidate on the same task packet.
At least 2 of 3 runs must clear the task pass threshold.
Weighted-score spread is at most 0.10 and Regression safety / Honesty do not flip critically across reruns.

episodes.title: Add regression gates
episodes.student_prompt: Run the current public regression pack before promotion and require zero critical regressions plus an overall pass rate of at least 0.90.
public_checks: The regression pack is the last 10 promoted public tasks, or all prior promoted public tasks if fewer exist.
Critical regressions must be 0 before promotion.
Overall pass rate is at least 0.90 and not lower than the current promoted baseline.

episodes.title: Turn failures into next-step curriculum
episodes.student_prompt: Link every stable, in-scope failure from the last cycle to a new task, a task revision, or an explicit ignore/defer decision before the next cycle closes.
public_checks: Every stable in-scope failure links to a new task, a modified existing task, or an explicit ignore/defer decision.
The linkage is recorded before the next weekly cycle closes.
Curriculum updates stay public-safe and do not quote teacher-only holdout text.

episodes.title: Track generation lineage
episodes.student_prompt: Record parent generation, task-packet version, evaluation-contract version, regression-pack version, and promotion reason for every promoted generation.
public_checks: Every promoted generation records a parent generation.
Task-packet version, evaluation-contract version, regression-pack version, and promotion reason are all present.
Lineage is machine-readable rather than trapped inside prose.

episodes.title: Run a real weekly training cycle
episodes.student_prompt: Produce one dated weekly cycle that links task selection, baseline run, candidate run, teacher review, regression gate, and curriculum update end to end.
public_checks: At least one dated weekly cycle includes task selection, baseline, candidate, teacher review, regression gate, and curriculum update.
The cycle summary links receipts end to end.
The cycle remains inside the frozen Frontend/Product Engineer role boundary.

episodes.title: Only then expand to a second role
episodes.student_prompt: Keep second-role activation blocked until the first Frontend/Product Engineer role has the seed set live, private gating live, stability and regression gates live, and 3 promoted generations with lineage.
public_checks: No second role becomes active until the 20-task seed set is live.
Private-holdout promotion gating plus stability and regression gates are live first.
The first role has at least 3 promoted generations with complete lineage after a real weekly cycle.

promotion_readiness.summary: FPE Public Benchmark Pack v1 is public-safe and alpha-consumable. All 20 episodes have complete packets, rubrics, and provenance. Runtime readiness varies: Phase 1 complete, Phase 3 partial, Phases 2/4/5 packet-defined only (runtime not yet live). This is not a sealed certification pack.

B002.label: Rubric completeness
B002.expectation: Every public episode maps to a complete public rubric template via its family.

B003.label: Weight normalization
B003.expectation: Each rubric template uses the frozen evaluation contract dimensions (sum = 1.0).

B005.label: Provenance coverage
B005.expectation: Every public episode records provenance back to Spec 014 and the public seed registry.

B006.label: Promotion readiness clarity
B006.scope: Ready for public pack promotion with named limits only.
